# Test Fixtures

Golden test fixtures for `CassetteDSPTests`. Generated by `make_fixtures.py`
using the same Python math as `analyze_master2.py` — the Swift tests assert
that the native implementations agree within the stated tolerances.

## Files

| File | Contents | Size |
|---|---|---|
| `make_fixtures.py` | Generator script (run to regenerate) | — |
| `expected.json` | Ground-truth values + tolerances | — |
| `chirp_fixture.wav` | Up-chirp at known offset in white noise | ~300 KB |
| `sounder_fixture.wav` | Schroeder multitone + silence at known SNR | ~570 KB |
| `clip_fixture.wav` | Clipped 1 kHz sine (float32) | ~190 KB |

## Regenerate

```bash
python3 Tests/fixtures/make_fixtures.py
# from the CassetteDSP package root
```

## Fixture contracts

- **chirp_fixture**: up-chirp at sample 96000 (2 s lead-in @ 48 kHz), tolerance ±64 samples.
- **sounder_fixture**: Schroeder 64-tone at -40 dBFS noise; expected SNR median ~50.6 dB,
  noise floor ~-40 dBFS. Swift tolerance ±2 dB on both.
- **clip_fixture**: float32 WAV, clipped at ±0.99. Expected clip fraction ~0.542 (tolerance ±10% relative).
